title: uralepis capensis
side_effects: Uralepis capensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. Prolonged or excessive use of the plant can also lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Furthermore, improper preparation or dosing of Uralepis capensis may cause cardiovascular issues, such as rapid heartbeat, hypertension, or even heart palpitations. In severe cases, misuse of the plant can result in kidney or liver damage, highlighting the importance of proper use and supervision.
